flags.concern: Video-editor navigation: seeding `?timeline=` from `settings.lastTimelineId` without validating it against `timelines.data` can strand the page on a deleted timeline instead of falling back to the first live timeline or auto-create. In this repo `VideoEditorPage` stops its auto-select logic as soon as any `timelineId` is present, while `SupabaseDataProvider.loadTimeline()` and `loadAssetRegistry()` quietly return default empty data for missing rows rather than failing fast, so the plan's stated 'provider failure path' does not actually recover this case.
flags.evidence: `src/tools/video-editor/pages/VideoEditorPage.tsx:210-248` bails the auto-select effect on any truthy `timelineId`; `src/tools/video-editor/data/SupabaseDataProvider.ts:51-70` and `src/tools/video-editor/data/SupabaseDataProvider.ts:175-186` default missing timeline rows to empty config/registry; `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineQueries.ts:6-24` just consumes that provider data and does not add page-level recovery.
